fact: March 17 is exactly 76 days into the year, which means there are 289 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this date typically falls during spring's arrival - trees are waking up from their winter nap and sap is rushing through their branches!
The sun rises and sets at almost exactly the same time all around the world on March 17 because it's so close to the spring equinox - day and night are nearly equal!
Earthworms often become very active around this time of year in temperate regions, squirming up from deep soil as the ground warms up - some can stretch 14 inches long!
March 17 falls during Pisces season in astrology, making it one of the most 'watery' days of the year - perfect for fish-like daydreaming!
In Antarctica, March 17 marks the beginning of winter darkness as the sun starts disappearing for months at a time!
Maple trees in cold climates are often pumping out their sweetest sap right around this date - one tree can produce 10 gallons of sap in a single day!
When there's a full moon on March 17, it creates some of the most dramatic spring tides of the year!
Migrating birds in North America are often flying overhead in massive flocks around March 17 - some sandhill cranes travel in groups of 10,000 birds!
In many places, March 17 typically brings the first real thunderstorms of spring as warm and cold air masses crash into each other!
The daylight on this date lasts almost exactly 12 hours in most places, making it one of the most 'balanced' days of the entire year!
Frogs and toads often start their loudest mating calls around March 17 in temperate zones - a single pond can sound like a monster truck rally at night!
